title: Extract Slow Requests from Access Log
description: Parse a tab-separated log where each line contains a request path and its response time in milliseconds. Find all requests that exceed the given threshold, returning them as a list of (path, ms) tuples sorted by response time in descending order.
constraints: Each log line is tab-separated: path\tlatency_ms
Return tuples sorted by latency descending
Return empty list if no requests exceed the threshold

examples.explanation: Only /api/v1/orders (2300ms) and /api/v1/reports (890ms) exceed the 500ms threshold. They are returned sorted by latency descending.

starter_code.python:
def slow_requests(log_content: str, threshold_ms: int = 500) -> list[tuple[str, int]]:
    pass

test_cases.description: All requests below threshold returns empty list
Custom threshold of 100ms, two paths exceed it
